title: AWS ECR: skopeo inspect fails because of 403
body:
skopeo inspect does not work with AWS ECR as the docker registry seems to return 403 when retrieving the tags.

```
skopeo inspect --creds=AWS:`cat token` docker://$AWS_ACCOUNT.dkr.ecr.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/NAME:TAG
time="2019-09-19T20:46:48Z" level=fatal msg="Error determining repository tags: Invalid status code returned when fetching tags list 403 (Forbidden)"
```

While skopeo works without problems as it doesn't need to list the tags.
```
skopeo copy --src-creds=AWS:`cat token` docker://$AWS_ACCOUNT.dkr.ecr.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/NAME:TAG dir:temp
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:xxx
Copying blob sha256:xxx
Copying config sha256:xxx
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

From my understand this is caused by https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/c4b0c7ce05cd601e62dfe8ecfde4fed42062dbdd/cmd/skopeo/inspect.go#L180 only gracefully allowing 401 (for IBM Bluemix) but not 403. 

Would it be fine to allow 403 as well?

comments.body:
Sure, if it is necessary — but we’ve had at least _some_ successful users of the library before, so I’d expect that this has worked previously.

Is this a new behavior change? Or is, perhaps, the credential token restricted so that it’s not allowed to list tags, or something like that? (Supporting less-privileged accounts would still be a good enough reason to make this optional, I’d just like to understand what is actually going on, if possible.)
(Also, as far as code is concerned, the error detection should be moved to c/image so that all callers benefit.)

I did some further testing with other AWS accounts and IAM settings. This was caused by not allowing the user the Action "ecr:ListImages".

Not sure how important it is for others to still use skopeo inspect while not giving the users/roles in AWS IAM the action "ecr:ListImages". In our accounts I just added the action.

Feel free to close if you don't see any benefit to change it.
Thanks, that’s very helpful. I’ve created #738 to not fail fatally in this case. Could you verify that it works as expected, please?
